creator: fernape@FreeBSD.org
text:
I found the faulting process:

pid 17387 (indexinfo), uid 0: exited on signal 11
pid 17388 (indexinfo), uid 0: exited on signal 11
pid 17389 (indexinfo), uid 0: exited on signal 11
pid 17390 (indexinfo), uid 0: exited on signal 11
pid 17391 (indexinfo), uid 0: exited on signal 11

So it seems that indexinfo breaks when trying to create the info pages.

This is confirmed by the fact that removing this line from the Makefile:

#INFO=          dynare

and adjusting pkg-plist accordingly results in a fine poudriere build without problems.

pkg does not report any information about the indexinfo invokation:

root@10_3amd64-default:/wrkdirs/usr/ports/math/dynare/work/pkg # pkg --debug install -f dynare-4.5.3.txz 
DBG(1)[45884]> pkg initialized
Updating local repository catalogue...
DBG(1)[45884]> PkgRepo: verifying update for local
DBG(1)[45884]> Pkgrepo, begin update of '/var/db/pkg/repo-local.sqlite'
DBG(1)[45884]> Fetch: fetching from: file:///packages/meta.txz with opts "i"
DBG(1)[45884]> Fetch: fetching from: file:///packages/packagesite.txz with opts "i"
local repository is up to date.
All repositories are up to date.
DBG(1)[45884]> want to get an advisory lock on a database
DBG(1)[45884]> Jobs> Adding file: dynare-4.5.3.txz
DBG(1)[45884]> solver: for package: octave cannot find provide for requirement: libgl2ps.so
Checking integrity...DBG(1)[45884]> check integrity for 1 items added
 done (0 conflicting)
The following 1 package(s) will be affected (of 0 checked):

New packages to be INSTALLED:
        dynare: 4.5.3

Number of packages to be installed: 1

The process will require 18 MiB more space.

Proceed with this action? [y/N]: y
DBG(1)[45884]> want to upgrade advisory to exclusive lock
[10_3amd64-default] [1/1] Installing dynare-4.5.3...
[10_3amd64-default] Extracting dynare-4.5.3: 100%
Segmentation fault
Segmentation fault
Segmentation fault
DBG(1)[45884]> release an exclusive lock on a database
DBG(1)[45884]> release an advisory lock on a database
root@10_3amd64-default:/wrkdirs/usr/ports/math/dynare/work/pkg # 

This seems to be a bug in print/indexinfo. Do you agree?
